text:
Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Instead of working on a whole project at once, teams use iterative development, breaking the work into small cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts one or two weeks.

At the start, the team organizes tasks from a backlog, which is a list of all work items. These tasks are described as user stories, simple explanations of a feature from the user’s viewpoint. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and challenges. This flexible system helps teams adapt quickly and deliver valuable results step by step.
